records.ety: [OE. currant, OF. curant, corant, p. pr. of curre, corre, F. courre, courir, to run, from L. currere; perh. akin to E. horse. Cf. Course, Concur, Courant, Coranto.]

quotes: Like the current fire, that renneth Upon a cord.
To chase a creature that was current then In these wild woods, the hart with golden horns.

defs: Now passing, as time; as, the current month.
Passing from person to person, or from hand to hand; circulating through the community; generally received; common; as, a current coin; a current report; current history.
quotes: That there was current money in Abraham's time is past doubt.
Your fire-new stamp of honor is scarce current.
His current value, which is less or more as men have occasion for him.

defs: Fitted for general acceptance or circulation; authentic; passable.
quotes: O Buckingham, now do I play the touch To try if thou be current gold indeed.

defs: A flowing or passing; onward motion. Hence: A body of fluid moving continuously in a certain direction; a stream; esp., the swiftest part of it; as, a current of water or of air; that which resembles a stream in motion; as, a current of electricity.
quotes: Two such silver currents, when they join, Do glorify the banks that bound them in.
The surface of the ocean is furrowed by currents, whose direction . . . the navigator should know.

defs: General course; ordinary procedure; progressive and connected movement; as, the current of time, of events, of opinion, etc.
